description: Full-stack app that processes sales meeting transcripts, categorizes them with an LLM (Gemma 4), and displays metrics in an interactive dashboard.

features.name: bootstrap
features.title: Bootstrap: Environment and Scaffolding
features.description: Install Docker (if missing), create .env.example, docker-compose.yml, Dockerfile, backend skeleton (FastAPI + SQLAlchemy), frontend skeleton (React+Vite), and verify docker compose up --build works. This is the foundation for all other features.
acceptance: Docker and Docker Compose are installed and running (or confirmed already present)
.env.example exists with all required variables documented
docker-compose.yml defines services: app and db (PostgreSQL 15)
Dockerfile builds a single container with Python backend and Node frontend build stage
backend/main.py starts FastAPI and serves /docs
backend/config.py defines settings (DB connection string, env vars) using pydantic-settings
backend/database.py creates SQLAlchemy engine, session factory, and Base
backend/models.py defines empty Client model placeholder (filled in feature 2)
backend/requirements.txt includes fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy, pandas, httpx, pydantic, pydantic-settings, pytest, psycopg2-binary
backend/tests/__init__.py exists
frontend/ has a working React+Vite app with package.json that builds to static files
docker compose up --build starts without errors and app is reachable at localhost:8000
init.sh passes all checks (steps 1-7 green)

features.name: project_scaffolding
features.title: Project Scaffolding (merged into bootstrap)
acceptance: Feature merged into feature 0 (bootstrap)

features.name: etl_pipeline
features.title: ETL Pipeline
features.description: Read CSV, clean data with pandas, extract signals with regex, and load into PostgreSQL. Runs automatically on first startup if table is empty.
acceptance: backend/models.py defines Client model with all fields: id, name, email, phone, meeting_date, vendor, closed (bool), transcription, sector, size, inquiry_volume, channel, source, integrations, tone, usage_type, pain, concreteness, categorized (bool, default false)
backend/etl/cleaner.py reads CSV, parses dates to datetime, handles nulls and duplicates
backend/etl/loader.py loads cleaned data into PostgreSQL clients table (categorization fields left empty, populated later by LLM)
ETL runs on startup only if table is empty (idempotent)
backend/tests/test_etl.py covers: CSV cleaning, date parsing, null handling, duplicate removal

features.name: categorizer_prompts_validator
features.title: Categorizer Prompts and Validator
features.description: LLM prompts with structured schema and few-shot examples for each dimension, plus category validation against predefined lists. ALL dimensions are categorized by the LLM (no regex extraction).
acceptance: backend/categorizer/prompts.py builds structured prompts with schema and few-shot examples for each dimension (sector, size, inquiry_volume, channel, source, integrations, tone, usage_type, pain, concreteness)
backend/categorizer/prompts.py extracts ALL dimensions from transcription via LLM — no regex extraction
backend/categorizer/validator.py validates returned categories against predefined lists from docs/domain.md, maps invalid to 'Otros'
backend/tests/test_categorizer.py covers: valid category passes, invalid category mapped, malformed JSON handled, empty response handled

features.name: categorizer_client
features.title: Categorizer Gemma Client
features.description: Async HTTP client for Gemma 4 API using httpx. Handles retries, timeouts, and malformed JSON responses gracefully.
acceptance: backend/categorizer/gemma_client.py makes async HTTP calls to Gemma 4 API with httpx
Malformed JSON responses are handled gracefully (record marked for retry, no crash)
backend/tests/test_gemma_client.py covers: successful call, timeout handling, malformed JSON, empty response, retry logic

features.name: categorizer_processor
features.title: Categorizer Batch Processor
features.description: Batch orchestration that processes uncategorized records in groups of 50, marks categorized=true after each batch, and supports resume on restart.
acceptance: backend/categorizer/processor.py processes batches of 50 records, marks categorized=true after each batch
Processor only processes WHERE categorized = false (resume on restart)
Background processing does not block API or dashboard
backend/tests/test_processor.py covers: batch processing, categorized flag update, resume after restart, empty table handling

features.name: api_clients_endpoints
features.title: API Client Endpoints
features.description: Pydantic response schemas and REST endpoints for clients: filters, search, and pagination. All queries run against categorized data only. Note: schemas defined here (ClientResponse, ClientListResponse, StatusResponse, MetricResponse) are shared by feature 7 (metrics endpoints).
acceptance: backend/api/schemas.py defines ClientResponse with all model fields serialized correctly
backend/api/schemas.py defines ClientListResponse with items, total, limit, offset
backend/api/schemas.py defines StatusResponse with total, categorized, progress, is_complete
backend/api/schemas.py defines MetricResponse with generic structure for metric data (used by feature 7)
All schemas use Pydantic v2 with proper field types and validators
GET /clients supports filters: sector, size, volume, source, channel, vendor, closed, date range
GET /clients?search=term searches across client name and email
Filters are combinable (AND logic)
GET /clients supports pagination with limit and offset
backend/tests/test_schemas.py covers: valid data serialization, missing fields, type coercion
backend/tests/test_api_clients.py covers: no filters, single filter, combined filters, search, empty results, pagination

features.name: api_metrics_endpoints
features.title: API Metrics Endpoints
features.description: REST endpoints for metrics aggregations. All queries run against categorized data only.
acceptance: backend/api/schemas.py defines MetricResponse with generic structure for metric data
GET /metrics/close-rate-by-sector returns close rate per sector (closed=1 by sector / total by sector)
GET /metrics/close-rate-by-vendor-sector returns heatmap-ready data (closed=1 by (vendor, sector) / total by (vendor, sector))
GET /metrics/close-rate-by-source returns close rate per acquisition source (closed=1 by source / total by source)
GET /metrics/pain-distribution-by-sector returns count of each pain category per sector
GET /metrics/close-rate-by-concreteness returns close rate per language concreteness level
GET /metrics/sector-distribution returns count by sector / total
GET /metrics/avg-volume-by-sector returns average monthly inquiry volume grouped by sector
GET /metrics/integrations-distribution returns count of each integration type
GET /metrics/close-rate-by-channel returns close rate per main contact channel
GET /metrics/temporal-evolution returns leads and closes grouped by month
GET /metrics/top-sectors-by-volume-rate returns sectors ranked by avg_volume * close_rate
GET /metrics/status returns total, categorized count, progress percentage, is_complete boolean
Metrics are calculated only on categorized records (partial data supported)
Records with 'Not specified' in a dimension are excluded from metrics that depend on that dimension (see docs/domain.md Data Exclusion Rule)
backend/tests/test_api_metrics.py covers: all metric endpoints, zero records edge case

features.name: dashboard_filters
features.title: Dashboard Filters Panel
features.description: React filters panel that allows selecting sector, vendor, source, channel, closed status, and date range. Changing filters updates all charts and table in real time.
acceptance: Filters panel allows selecting: sector, vendor, source, channel, closed status, date range
Changing filters updates all charts and table in real time
Filters are passed as query params to API endpoints
App builds and runs without errors via docker compose up

features.name: dashboard_kpi_cards
features.title: Dashboard KPI Cards
features.description: KPI cards displaying top-level metrics: total leads, overall close rate, top sector, top vendor.
acceptance: KPI cards display: total leads, overall close rate, top sector, top vendor
KPI cards update when filters change
App builds and runs without errors via docker compose up

features.name: dashboard_client_table
features.title: Dashboard Client Table
features.description: Searchable and paginated client table component with categorization progress bar.
acceptance: Client table is searchable by name/email and paginated
Categorization progress bar shows percentage while background processing runs
App builds and runs without errors via docker compose up

features.name: dashboard_charts_basic
features.title: Dashboard Charts (Basic)
features.description: Core chart visualizations using Chart.js: bar charts, donut, and stacked bars.
acceptance: Bar chart shows close rate by sector (sorted descending)
Donut chart shows sector distribution
Stacked bar chart shows pain distribution by sector (100% stacked)
Bar chart shows close rate by acquisition source with global average reference line
Bar chart shows close rate by language concreteness with highlighted gap
Charts respond to filter changes

features.name: dashboard_charts_advanced
features.title: Dashboard Charts (Advanced)
features.description: Advanced chart visualizations using Chart.js: heatmap, dual line chart, treemap, and scatter plot.
acceptance: Heatmap shows vendor × industry close rates
Line chart shows leads and closes over time (monthly, dual line)
Treemap or horizontal bars shows top requested integrations
Scatter plot shows volume × close rate by sector with labeled points
Charts respond to filter changes

features.name: ci_cd_pipeline
features.title: CI/CD Pipeline
features.description: GitHub Actions workflows for linting, testing, and Docker build verification. Runs on every push and PR.
acceptance: .github/workflows/ci.yml defines a workflow triggered on push and pull_request
CI workflow runs Python linting (ruff) and formatting check (black --check)
CI workflow runs frontend linting (eslint/prettier)
CI workflow builds Docker image with docker compose build
CI workflow runs pytest inside the container: docker compose exec app pytest backend/tests/ -v
CI workflow runs frontend build: npm run build inside the container
Workflow fails fast if any step fails (no partial green)
.github/workflows/deploy.yml defines deployment workflow for Railway on push to main (placeholder for final deploy)

features.name: integration_and_polish
features.title: Integration and Polish
features.description: End-to-end verification, README with setup instructions, architecture documentation, and final cleanup.
acceptance: docker compose up --build runs from clean state without errors
ETL + categorization run automatically on first startup
Dashboard shows real data with working charts and filters
All pytest tests pass: docker compose exec app pytest backend/tests/ -v
README.md contains: project description, local setup instructions (docker compose up --build), architecture overview, key design decisions and trade-offs
README.md should be written in Spanish
No debug print() statements, no TODOs without context, no temp files
